method: Web-research; metric names verified against official docs / source via WebFetch. Confidence high = verbatim from official metrics reference; med = official prose; low = uncertain spelling. NOTE: these are LIVE runtime telemetry a pilot must scrape — NOT downloadable public datasets.
key_finding: Cold-start/model-load and autoscaling ARE first-class Prometheus metrics — but ONLY in Ray Serve (orchestration layer). The LLM engines (vLLM/SGLang/TGI/Triton) expose queue, KV-usage, TTFT, tail-latency, success natively but NO model-load/cold-start. Request migration is exposed as a metric NOWHERE (only preemption + KV-transfer-byte proxies). Therefore most frontier signals are RECONSTRUCTABLE from a metric-scraping pilot; migration is the one that needs custom instrumentation.

stacks.migration_metric_present: partial — vllm:num_preemptions_total (preemption, not request migration); vllm:external_prefix_cache_hits covers KV-offload transfer hits
stacks.gpu_metric_present: partial — kv_cache_usage_perc only; no raw SM-util/power (use DCGM)
stacks.notes: Most complete LLM metrics set. NO model-load/startup/warmup/cold-start metric (verified). KV-eviction-adjacent histograms (kv_block_lifetime, kv_block_idle_before_evict) are rare and useful. request_success_total{finished_reason=stop/length/abort} gives success-by-reason; no explicit timeout/failure counter.

stacks.gpu_metric_present: partial — token_usage/num_used_tokens (KV fill); no raw GPU util
stacks.notes: cache_hit_rate exposed directly as a gauge (more convenient than vLLM's counters). NO model-load/cold-start, NO preemption/migration, NO explicit success/failure/timeout counter in the documented set (would need custom instrumentation/log parsing).

THE ONLY audited stack exposing cold-start/model-load AND autoscaling as first-class metrics. As an orchestration layer it has NO LLM-internal signals (no KV cache, prefix-hit, TTFT, prefill/decode, GPU util) — those come from the wrapped engine. No request-migration metric.

stacks.autoscaling_metric_present: partial — via Knative KPA, not KServe-native
stacks.notes: KServe's own metrics = 4 step-latency histograms. NO unified metric set — each model server emits its own. Queue/autoscaling come from Knative (revision_request_count, KPA autoscaler), not KServe. For an LLM pilot the meaningful signals come from the underlying runtime (e.g. vLLM).

stacks.queue_metric_present: partial — no explicit queue-depth; request_in_progress is closest proxy
stacks.notes: 1.2+ uses bentoml_service_* (older 1.1.x used bentoml_api_server_*/bentoml_runner_*). Success/failure via http_response_code label. NO LLM-internal signals, cold-start, GPU, or native queue-depth. LLM signals require the underlying engine.

Strong queue + prefill/decode coverage. NOTABLE GAP: NO dedicated TTFT histogram (derive from queue_duration + prefill or measure client-side). NO prefix/KV cache-hit metric, NO KV usage gauge, NO GPU, NO model-load/warmup metric.

stacks.migration_metric_present: partial — num_remote_read/write_bytes track KV-cache transfer (substrate of cross-instance migration), not request migration per se
stacks.notes: BEST-IN-CLASS cache-hit + KV-transfer telemetry: hit RATE directly (gauge + per-request histogram) + token counters + usage gauges. Surfaces through vLLM /metrics when co-located. A cache library, not a server — no queue/serving-latency/GPU/autoscaling/cold-start.

stacks.migration_metric_present: partial — Transfer Engine exposes transfer/throughput stats (KV movement), metric names undocumented
stacks.cache_hit_metric_present: yes (store-side cache-reuse signals) but exact names unverified
stacks.tail_latency_metric_present: partial — docs mention latency stats, no named histogram
key_metrics: (exact Prometheus names NOT published; GET /metrics + /metrics/summary; categories: MEMORY/SSD_CURRENT_CACHED_OBJECTS, *_HIT_RATE, transfer stats gated by MC_TE_METRIC=1)
stacks.notes: Has a Prometheus /metrics endpoint and store-side cache-reuse + transfer signals, but verbatim metric names NOT published. CONFIDENCE LOW — a pilot must scrape a live endpoint to enumerate. Least-documented stack.

stacks.cache_hit_metric_present: partial — nv_cache_* is RESPONSE cache (exact-match), NOT KV/prefix-cache hit rate; KV reuse exposed as block usage not hit-rate
stacks.notes: ONLY audited stack with first-class GPU hardware metrics (nv_gpu_utilization/memory/power) AND explicit request_success + request_failure counters. TTFT via nv_inference_first_response_histogram_ms. nv_cache_* is response cache (NOT prefix/KV) — common trap. NO model-load/warmup metric. Standalone trtllm-serve had no native Prometheus endpoint as of mid-2025.

reconstructable_only_via_orchestration_w: autoscaling (Ray Serve autoscaling_* ; KServe via Knative KPA)
needs_custom_instrumentation_no_metric_a: per_request_timeout_label (only success/failure counters; explicit timeout label needs custom)
signal_reconstructability_summary.caveat: All of the above are LIVE runtime telemetry — a pilot deployment must scrape Prometheus to capture them. NONE is a downloadable public dataset; they answer 'reconstructable from a pilot', not 'available publicly today'.
